goal: Implement the first GAP-012 replayable trace evidence slice for runtime-card without broadening closeout authority.
objective: Add an opt-in runtime-card --trace-out lane that writes replayable JSONL event evidence using the existing agent-run-event/v1 substrate, while keeping runtime-card advisory and artifact-backed.
feedbackSignal: GAP-012 says significant harness runs lack default replayable JSONL/session evidence, making final state claims hard to replay or debug.
rootOperationalFailure: runtime-card can summarize current state and write artifacts, but it does not currently emit an event-level trace artifact that records command start, verifier/projection status, artifact writes, final status, stop reason, or failure classification.
plannedDurableImprovement: Introduce a small trace writer deep module and wire runtime-card --trace-out to append hash-chained agent-run-event/v1 JSONL records to the canonical artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl stream for start, evidence/projection, artifact writes when present, and final status or error.
forbiddenFiles: unrelated generated, cache, or runtime output artifacts
nonGoals: Do not make trace output mandatory for every command in this slice.
Do not add trace output to pr-closeout or verify-work in this slice unless the intent is revised and re-reviewed.
Do not change runtime-card recommendation behavior or make traces closeout proof.
Do not persist prompts, raw command output, secrets, environment values, or bulky session transcripts.
Do not claim GAP-012 is fully closed until pr-closeout, verify-work style commands, and default-emission policy are implemented or explicitly accepted as follow-ups by Judge/PM.
implementationApproach: Create a pure runtime-trace writer that accepts a repo root, a repo-relative canonical trace-out events path, and event descriptors, then derives the run id from artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl.

Constrain --trace-out to the canonical run-record event path pattern artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl; reject absolute paths, path traversal, non-events.jsonl basenames, and non-canonical directories as usage errors with exit 2 and no trace emission instead of writing undiscoverable traces.
Use the existing appendCanonicalEvent run-record primitive so event writes inherit temp-copy plus append plus atomic rename semantics and hash-chain validation.

Write or refresh the sibling terminal manifest at artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/manifest.json after runtime-card completes or fails after argument parsing so loadRunRecordBundle can replay the canonical run record end to end.

Emit line-delimited agent-run-event/v1 records with deterministic event types that fit the existing schema: phase for start/final status, precondition for input/evidence classification, artifact_write for --out/--evidence-out writes, error for failures, and degraded_mode when local evidence is advisory or external state remains unrefreshed.

Validate replayability by loading the emitted canonical run-record event stream with the existing event parser so every line validates against agent-run-event/v1 and prevEventHash/eventHash continuity.
Record stopReason, exitCode, command, context, evidencePath presence, artifact paths, and sanitized blocker/error summaries in payloads.
Update runtime-card CLI parsing and usage to accept --trace-out.
Add tests proving successful trace emission, failure trace emission, repo-bound path rejection, hash-chain continuity, and no trace file on parse-only usage errors.
Document the opt-in trace lane as advisory replay evidence, not delivery proof.
Record goal receipt/state updates that keep local trace evidence separate from push/PR/CI/review/Linear readiness.

expectedOutputContract.traceFile: repo-relative canonical JSONL file supplied by --trace-out, constrained to artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl
expectedOutputContract.runIdSource: derived from the parent directory name in artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl
events: precondition:passed|blocked for evidence/projection inputs and path validation
degraded_mode:passed when runtime-card runs from local/advisory evidence without live external refresh
error:failed plus phase:failed when runtime-card reaches runtime failure after argument parsing

eventMapping.runtime_failure: error:failed severity=error followed by phase:failed severity=error

expectedOutputContract.hashChain: Every event includes eventHash; every event after the first includes prevEventHash matching the prior eventHash.
expectedOutputContract.invalidTraceOutHandling: absolute, escaping, non-canonical, or non-events.jsonl --trace-out values are usage errors with exit 2 and no trace emission

validationGates: node --import tsx -e "import { loadRunRecordBundle } from './src/lib/contract/run-records.ts'; loadRunRecordBundle({ cwd: process.cwd(), runId: 'runtime-card-trace-test' });"

pnpm exec biome check src/lib/runtime-trace src/commands/runtime-card.ts src/commands/runtime-card-args.ts src/commands/runtime-card.test.ts docs/cli-reference.md ARCHITECTURE.md docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-026-gap-012-runtime-card-trace-out-intent.json

git diff --check -- src/lib/runtime-trace src/commands/runtime-card.ts src/commands/runtime-card-args.ts src/commands/runtime-card.test.ts docs/cli-reference.md ARCHITECTURE.md docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-026-gap-012-runtime-card-trace-out-intent.json
acceptanceCriteria: runtime-card accepts --trace-out only for canonical artifacts/agent-runs/<runId>/events.jsonl paths and writes discoverable JSONL trace evidence on successful execution.
runtime-card writes a sibling agent-run-manifest/v1 manifest so the emitted trace is replayable through canonical run-record loading, not only readable as a loose JSONL file.
runtime-card writes an error trace when execution reaches runtime failure after argument parsing.
runtime-card treats invalid --trace-out path values as usage errors with exit 2 and no trace emission.
Trace events validate against agent-run-event/v1 through the existing run-record parser and include status, severity, payload, eventHash, and hash-chain continuity.
Trace payloads include command, context, stopReason, exitCode, artifact refs where applicable, and sanitized error/blocker summaries without secrets or raw environment values.
Trace output remains opt-in and advisory; runtime-card does not treat trace presence as closeout, CI, review, Linear, or merge-readiness proof.
Trace-out paths cannot escape the repository root and cannot bypass canonical run-record discovery.
Documentation states this is the first GAP-012 runtime-card trace slice and does not close default trace emission for all significant commands.
Goal receipt records Project Brain inputs, audit freshness, validation evidence, reviewer artifacts, and branch-divergence blocker separately.
riskControls: Reuse agent-run-event/v1 instead of inventing a competing trace schema.
Reuse appendCanonicalEvent instead of implementing a second event append/hash-chain writer.
Keep the trace writer pure and small under a deep module.
Keep event payloads compact and redacted.
Do not write traces unless --trace-out is explicitly provided.
Preserve unrelated dirty and untracked worktree files.
blockedConditions: Existing agent-run-event/v1 schema cannot represent the required trace statuses without broad schema changes.
Repo-scoped trace-out path validation cannot be made deterministic.
Runtime-card tests cannot exercise both success and failure traces without brittle process-level shelling.
